generatedFrom: Hand-curated v1 aggregate lookup; roster ETL should replace aggregate counts once raw XLSX files are processed in the Google Cloud pipeline.

compliance.publicOutput: Aggregate archetypes only. No athlete names, photos, likenesses, finish times, or protected visual marks.
compliance.copyRule: Use conditional language and keep Olympic and Paralympic sport pairings equally prominent.

archetypes.name: The Striker
archetypes.summary: Quick decisions, clean angles, and a burst of team-first pressure.
aggregateFacts: Court-based Team USA pathways often reward spatial reads, repeat acceleration, and coordinated timing.
The paired Paralympic and Olympic court sports share tactical spacing while expressing movement through different mechanics.
narrativeBeats: Your inputs could align with a court archetype built around anticipation and fast release.
This pairing keeps wheelchair basketball and basketball side by side as equal expressions of spacing, timing, and touch.
The Striker may be associated with movement that turns small openings into decisive chances.
Across Team USA history, court sports have often made teamwork visible through quick, repeatable choices.

archetypes.name: The Flow
archetypes.summary: Long rhythm, controlled breath, and efficient motion through resistance.
aggregateFacts: Aquatic Team USA pathways often center rhythm, body line, and repeat power.
The Paralympic and Olympic swim pairing gives equal analytical depth to pace, water feel, and controlled momentum.
narrativeBeats: Your profile could align with a water archetype where rhythm matters as much as force.
This pairing treats para swimming and swimming as parallel studies in pace, line, and adaptation.
The Flow may be associated with calm repetition under pressure.
Team USA aquatic history offers a deep lane for stories about breath, timing, and sustained speed.

archetypes.name: The Spring
archetypes.summary: Coiled starts, fast turnover, and a clean shift from stillness to speed.
aggregateFacts: Sprint pathways often emphasize acceleration, stiffness, turnover, and finish-line composure.
The Paralympic and Olympic sprint pairing keeps explosive track movement structurally balanced.
narrativeBeats: Your inputs could align with an acceleration archetype: compact, reactive, and ready to go.
This pairing puts para athletics sprint and track sprint in the same spotlight for power, timing, and control.
The Spring may be associated with starts that convert patience into speed.
Team USA sprint stories often turn a fraction of a second into a full-body decision.

archetypes.name: The Aim
archetypes.summary: Quiet posture, steady hands, and precision under rising pressure.
aggregateFacts: Precision pathways often reward stillness, repeatable setup, and calm execution.
The para archery and archery pairing highlights control and focus with equivalent visual importance.
narrativeBeats: Your profile could align with a precision archetype where restraint is part of the power.
This pairing gives para archery and archery equal weight as sports of setup, hold, and release.
The Aim may be associated with pressure management and repeatable mechanics.
Team USA precision stories often live in the quiet seconds before motion.

archetypes.name: The Launch
archetypes.summary: Grounded force, rotational power, and the confidence to let it fly.
aggregateFacts: Throwing pathways often center leverage, ground force, and controlled release.
The para throws and Olympic throws pairing keeps power events represented with equal depth.
narrativeBeats: Your inputs could align with a power archetype that builds force from the ground up.
This pairing treats para shot put and Olympic throws as equal studies in leverage, rotation, and release.
The Launch may be associated with patient setup and sudden transfer.
Team USA throwing stories often make strength visible as timing, not just size.
